calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion To File Under Seal.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to file Exhibit A to the Emily Fowler Declaration in support of Safeway's Good Faith Settlement Determination under seal is granted. Exhibit A discloses the settlement amount. Safeway seeks confidentiality on the grounds that a promise of confidentiality is material to the settlement of the case, and that knowledge of the settlement amount would be misused in other cases or misinterpreted as an admission of fault or liability. Safeway's factual showing is thin; it offers no declaration from a person with knowledge about the latter propositions concerning potential misuses of knowledge of the settlement amount. But the court accepts that confidentiality is a material provision of this settlement agreement, and parties' resolution of their own disputes is of significant and indeed overriding public interest. The court therefore finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing Exhibit A to the Fowler Declaration; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (See Rules of Court, 2.550(d).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and outlines the documents to be sealed, including their respective titles, filing dates and transaction numbers as shown in the register of actions.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Jun-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF RANJEET DOSANJH's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Ranjeet Dosanjh moves to quash subpoenas issued by defendant Gardaworld to Dosanjh's mental health treatment providers. The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part.

Dosanjh's complaint alleges she was subject to sexual harassment at her place of employment, a bank inside of a Safeway. She contends that a Gardaworld employee who provided security at the store sexually harassed her and subjected her to unwanted and offensive sexual contact. The subpoenas seek all documents and records related to her psychiatric care or treatment from 2012 through the present.

The subpoenas are overbroad in that they seek treatment records from 2012 through the present. But Dosanjh seeks damages for "personal, medical and related care and treatment plaintiff has incurred and will continue to incur" (Compl. para. 133), and her prayer for damages includes a prayer for "medical and related expenses." (Compl. p. 29:18-19.) Dosanjh testified in deposition that she sought therapy as a result of the harassment and assault. (Depo. 149:3-9 [Ex. C to Cullman Dec.].) The court agrees with Dosanjh that her IIED claim and her description in the complaint and in her deposition of her reaction to the harassment present a straightforward response to a "simple sexual harassment claim" of the kind described in Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 840, that would not ordinarily put at issue a plaintiff's mental health history or alternative causes of claimed trauma. But by seeking damages for treatment including therapy, Dosanjh has put at issue the cost of her treatment and the issue of whether it was causally connected to the assault and harassment her complaint describes. The court grants the motion in part; Gardaworld may obtain the requested records from Better Help and Laurel Therapy Collective for the time period October 20, 2023 through the present only. Any party who receives a copy of any of the above records as a result of the subpoena is ordered not to use them except in connection with this case, not to disseminate them to any person except outside experts retained in connection with this case who have agreed to be bound by the restrictions in this sentence of the court's order, and to destroy them within 60 days of final disposition of this case.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
